children.text: A lot of people write a lot of things all over the Internet these days, plus you've got AI writing half the things that come out, well newsflash the AI was trained on things written before so it's not exactly new or fresh.  But anyways, I don't think a whole lot of the art of verbal composition and I think it shows.  Ultimately though I've decided to have three main points for this essay so I'll have to start with:
This exists to redeem the art of writing.
Two main points would be enough.

So if you've checked out this page already you've noticed I have a whole essay on what Crousian is under the ABOUT link (up top is the links).  Well, this is another Crousian essay, the second of its kind, really, in an official sense.  Every other essay can roughly be categorized as "attempting to write in English" and every other essay is in some sense a failure.  You might notice there isn't much of a "formula" for the put-together-ness of this paragraph, and that's one of the main Crousian elements.

I am here to, as I said in my bullet point, redeem the art of writing.  I have to say this to remind you what I am writing about, lest you would forget and think I was just traipsing around the lala land of my mind attempting to coerce you into following my 🚂 of 💭.  But what keeps you reading?  Well, the redemption.  The redemption keeps you reading.  But it isn't a redemption in the religious sense.  Even the word redemption needs redemption from time to time.  And Jesus Saves.  But that's just because his name means "my God is a Savior" -- whereas my own name, Jeremiah, means "God will Exalt", and I am here to exalt you for taking the time to read what I have spelled out.

What is the point of writing?  Well this can be variable; but if it's there to tax your attention and make you wish you were asleep, then it's not good.  This redemptive Crousian essay is here to give you the opportunity to be grateful that you were given eyes to read and taught the letters and symbols.  Here's to hoping it works. 🍻🥂
